title: Power-outage critical-load budget
summary: Allocate limited battery or generator capacity to communication, medical needs, safe lighting, and essential cooling before comfort loads.
content:
Allocate limited battery or generator capacity to communication, medical needs, safe lighting, and essential cooling before comfort loads.

Field sequence: List each device’s purpose, power demand, duty cycle, remaining energy, charging priority, and manual fallback, then protect an emergency reserve. Define the essential function, available energy, rated limits, hazards, and a manual fallback before connecting equipment for “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Inspect plugs, insulation, batteries, fuel, ventilation, moving parts, guards, moisture, and structural support for “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Disconnect or isolate the source before adjustment; use the correct tool, rating, polarity, protection, and dry working area for “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Test one small reversible step under observation before depending on the repair or power arrangement for “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Label status, limits, last test, remaining capacity, fault signs, and the next inspection for “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Power-outage critical-load budget”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help in “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Stop immediately for heat, swelling, smoke, sparks, burning smell, fuel leak, unusual noise, vibration, or repeated protective-device trips in “Power-outage critical-load budget”. Reduced fallback: If “load and battery inventory for “Power-outage critical-load budget”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.

title: Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone
summary: Prevent carbon-monoxide poisoning by keeping portable fuel-burning equipment outdoors and away from occupied openings.
content:
Prevent carbon-monoxide poisoning by keeping portable fuel-burning equipment outdoors and away from occupied openings.

Safe-use sequence: Never run a generator in a home, garage, shed, balcony enclosure, stairwell, or near doors, windows, or vents; use alarms and manufacturer instructions. Define the essential function, available energy, rated limits, hazards, and a manual fallback before connecting equipment for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Inspect plugs, insulation, batteries, fuel, ventilation, moving parts, guards, moisture, and structural support for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Disconnect or isolate the source before adjustment; use the correct tool, rating, polarity, protection, and dry working area for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Test one small reversible step under observation before depending on the repair or power arrangement for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Label status, limits, last test, remaining capacity, fault signs, and the next inspection for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help in “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Stop immediately for heat, swelling, smoke, sparks, burning smell, fuel leak, unusual noise, vibration, or repeated protective-device trips in “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”. Reduced fallback: If “load and battery inventory for “Portable generator carbon-monoxide exclusion zone”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.

title: Safe night-lighting hierarchy
summary: Provide enough light for exits, stairs, toilets, medicines, and hazards without introducing flame, trip, glare, or battery-waste risks.
content:
Provide enough light for exits, stairs, toilets, medicines, and hazards without introducing flame, trip, glare, or battery-waste risks.

Safe-use sequence: Place low-power lights at route changes and task points, preserve one emergency light, and keep candles away from children, animals, fabrics, sleep, and unattended areas. Define the essential function, available energy, rated limits, hazards, and a manual fallback before connecting equipment for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Inspect plugs, insulation, batteries, fuel, ventilation, moving parts, guards, moisture, and structural support for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Disconnect or isolate the source before adjustment; use the correct tool, rating, polarity, protection, and dry working area for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Test one small reversible step under observation before depending on the repair or power arrangement for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Label status, limits, last test, remaining capacity, fault signs, and the next inspection for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Safety limits: Official instructions and emergency services override “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”; do not delay evacuation or urgent help to complete the card. Do not work on live mains electricity, gas systems, pressurised vessels, damaged lithium batteries, or unstable structures without qualified help in “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Stop immediately for heat, swelling, smoke, sparks, burning smell, fuel leak, unusual noise, vibration, or repeated protective-device trips in “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”. Reduced fallback: If “load and battery inventory for “Safe night-lighting hierarchy”” is unavailable, use only a clean, labelled substitute that performs the same function; otherwise mark the missing capability and pause that step.
